algorithms.title: Acute Asthma/COPD Exacerbation Field Approach
algorithms.description: Sequence for assessing and managing an acute asthma or COPD exacerbation.
steps: Assess severity: ability to speak in full sentences, accessory muscle use, and wheeze intensity — remember a silent chest is more severe than a loud wheeze, not less.
For mild-moderate distress: administer bronchodilator therapy per protocol, position of comfort, monitor closely.
For severe distress or signs of impending failure (altered mental status, exhaustion, silent chest): escalate rapidly, prepare for ventilatory support, transport rapidly.
If known or suspected COPD: apply titrated oxygen targeting approximately 88-92% SpO2 — do not withhold oxygen from a hypoxic patient out of hypoxic-drive concerns.
Reassess frequently; treat a quieting wheeze with worsening distress as a warning sign, not reassurance.
